TRANSCRIPT
Welcome to Launching Your Leverage - the Secrets of Leverage and Scale. What
this training is all about is about growing your business, and more importantly growing
your business without growing the time that you spend in your business.
This isn't about getting on the treadmill, and working harder, and harder, and
harder, and harder, and harder, and trying to make more money. It's about the opposite
of that. It's about continuing to make more as you spend less time in your business, and
you spend less time doing the things that you don't love to do.
Now, one quick caveat here, I'm going to use the term, as we go through this, I'm
going to use the term, "Make more," quite a bit. I've been in this business long enough to
know that the people going through my trainings, there are times in their lives when
they're more focused on making money, and there are times in their lives and their
business when they're more focused on making an impact.
You might fall into one or the other of those camps, or both of them. When I say,
"Make more," you can think about making more sales, making more income, making
more profit, or you could think about serving more, and helping more, and making a
bigger impact, which ever. I'm going to put them under that umbrella of make more. This
is all about growing your business to make more, without working harder.
There's enough challenges in life and in business that you don't have to go and
create a business that becomes like a living hell, where it just makes your life harder, and
it burns you out. You don't need to do that. There are decisions to make, there's things
you can do that could make everything a lot harder, or make things a lot easier.
This training's about helping you make the right choices. I'm going to tell you right
now, your choices aren't going to be 100% perfect. No one is perfect. You're going to make
mistakes. You're going to screw this up at times, but I want to prepare you, as well as I
can, to make those decisions such that you create a business that you love, and that
creates a life that you love.
I've made plenty of mistakes over the years, but I've mostly gotten this right. I don't
know exactly why that is. I know I tend to be pretty analytical, and take my time making

different. Let me just tell you what they are.
Leverage is this idea of ... I mean it's the idea of a lever. You have a lever… there's a
phrase that is, “Small hinges swing big doors.” What are those hinges? What are those
small tweaks that you can make in your business that will create a big impact, and that
will create a great, great big impact without you putting in a lot of extra time?
There's lots of different type of leverage. There's financial leverage. I'm going to be
talking about time leverage, specifically your time leverage, so that you can create greater
results without putting in a lot of extra time. That's what this whole idea is. It's like where
can you put in a little bit of time? Either it's one time, a one-time effort, or it's a small
amount on an ongoing basis, and create lots of results? That's what leverage is.
Now, scale is this idea of where it can continue to grow, where your business can
grow, and has a big runway where it can just get a lot bigger. Continue to get much, much
bigger. There's things that will scale, and things that won't scale. There's things that have
leverage, and things that inherently won't have leverage.
If you're coaching people, one on one, that is inherently not a leveraged business,
and it's also not a scalable business either, not until you can replicate yourself, and start
to train other people to coach with your methodologies, so you can hire coaches. Now, you
can start to get leverage, and it can start to scale. There are levels of scale.
For instance, at one point I had a membership program, sort of a membership site,
and that membership site was for a specific piece of software that people use to analyze
mutual funds. It was very, very, super specialized. It was super specialized in how it
actually approached investing in mutual funds.
The entire universe of users of that software, I'm going to guess, somewhere around
500. That's just a wild guess. There's this software, and it was really a software service
way back in the day where people would get the software, and they would pay for the data,
and they would use this software every single day to look at the market, at these mutual
funds.
I had this membership site for people that use that software. Let's say there was
approximately 500 people that use that software, 300 of them were members of my
community on my membership site, so it was really nice. I charged $100 a year to access

this, so I made $30,000 a year.

Almost everyone renewed every single year, so it was like $30,000 a year, with

Now, my current business will scale to some level because more and more people

how to lose weight - that would have a much, much bigger audience.
That would have just a much bigger possibility. I think that business would scale

much bigger than my current business where I'm teaching entrepreneurs how to start and
grow their online businesses. Different markets are different in how much they'll scale,

This is an important piece. Leverage and scale are very intertwined. Many of the
things that will give you leverage, will allow you to scale. Many of the things that will
allow you to scale will give you leverage, so they're very, very intertwined. They're not the
same, and I hope you get this as I walk through this. They're not the same, but they're
very, very intertwined.
You have to think about what you want to scale, and what you want to leverage. For
instance, this is an important point that a lot of people miss. I have this program called
my Platinum Plus Program. It's a high-end mastermind. Currently, it costs $30,000 to
join that program. I only have 50 spots available in it. It's a very nice program. If you do
the math, $30,000 times 50 spots, I think it comes out to $1.5 million. I'm pretty sure
that's what it comes out to.
I don't have a ton of time into that every year, so it is leveraged, it's a leveraged
thing, but it won't scale because I've only got 50 spots. Over time, I do raise the price.
Maybe by the time you hear this recording it might cost a lot more than $30,000, but it's
not going to scale in a huge way.
It will not grow 10 times bigger than what it is, in terms of dollars, unless I radically
increase the price, or I decide to radically increase the number of spots available, both of
which are highly unlikely. One of the things that makes the program so powerful, and it is
very, very, very powerful, is that we only have those 50 spots.
That business is leveraged. I certainly make it a lot of money for the amount of time
I have in it, but it's not really going to scale, or the dollars aren't really going to scale, let's
put it that way. The dollars don't scale, but the impact on my business does scale because
that Platinum Plus mastermind isn't just a mastermind for the people in the program, it's
also a mastermind for me, and it now has many of the top leaders, thought leaders,
experts in my industry.
By being around them, I not only get the benefit of their ideas in the mastermind,

continue to level up my skills, and capabilities, and performance, and results. If I look at

my business since I started that Platinum Plus mastermind, and it's been seven years
now, I've just about ... I'm closing in on tripling my business.
That triple, that's every year. That's not just one time. If I look at the value that that
program has brought to me, in addition just to the dollars coming in, it has pushed me to
... It's been seven years, and I've increased my business. It's been many, many millions of

It doesn't scale in terms of direct dollars, but it scales in terms of overall business

levels on this. Don't just think about dollars, or don't just think about impact. When
you're looking at a program, think about what else it could bring you.
When I look at a program, I'm thinking about okay, what are the dollars this could
bring in? What's the impact I can make? Also, what can I learn? Am I going to have fun?
Does this have strategic value beyond just this project? Will it set me up for other bigger
projects? Are there more leverage projects? Are there more scalable projects?
Then finally, I want to leave you in this intro on one idea, and that's this idea of
unique ability. That's something I learned from Dan Sullivan. Unique ability is this idea
of, it's something that when you're doing it, time just disappears. You're so uniquely good
at it, so uniquely ... Let's call it your genius zone. You're so fantastic at it that time almost
seems to disappear. It's just effortless for you to do, but other people are amazed at how
you can do it.
We all have these unique ability areas, so ultimately what I'm talking about in this
entire training, especially the leverage part, is how to get you to spend more time in that
area of your unique ability, where it just comes easy to you, but other people are just
shocked by the results you get, and by the things you can create.
If you can pare back your involvement in your business so that you are increasingly
spending more time in your unique ability, that right there is true leverage. That is true
leverage, and ultimately it'll probably bring you to scale as well. In the next section, we're
going to go deep on leverage, so head right on over to the next section where we go into
